title: campium subsimplex
intro: Campium subsimplex, also known as the 'Wood Sorrel', is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Oxalidaceae. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary tract infections and kidney stones due to its high concentration of oxalic acid, which acts as a diuretic and helps to dissolve kidney stones. The health benefits of Campium subsimplex include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as an antiseptic. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in salves and poultices to treat skin conditions and wounds.
